subject: Biology
title: Fertilization in Plants
description: A micro-lesson that makes Fertilization in Plants usable.

hook.text: When a pollen grain lands on the pistil, it grows a tube down to the ovule to deliver sperm cells.

buildup.text: The pollen tube can take hours to grow, pushing through tissue to reach the egg cell.

discovery.text: In flowering plants, double fertilization occurs—one sperm fertilizes the egg, another creates the endosperm.

twist.text: The endosperm is the starchy food supply for the developing embryo—it's what makes rice and wheat nutritious.

climax.text: Double fertilization is unique to flowering plants and helped make them Earth's dominant plant group.

punchline.text: Two fertilizations in one visit. Maximum efficiency.

quiz.question: What does double fertilization produce besides the embryo?
